title: Trim and reject empty DTO fields
scenario: Boundary strings often contain whitespace. Normalize only after checking the DTO field exists, then reject empty normalized values explicitly.
instructions: Update TryFrom<RegisterUserDto> to trim both fields and return EmptyEmail or EmptyDisplayName when a trimmed field is empty.

hints.body: Keep the Converting DTOs into domain commands code active and update TryFrom<RegisterUserDto> to trim both fields and return EmptyEmail or EmptyDisplayName when a trimmed field is empty.
Use the Rust API named in this lesson instead of adding a side helper; the previous arc code should stay in the main solution.
One complete solution for Trim and reject empty DTO fields is shown below.

completionExplanation: Trim and reject empty DTO fields now extends the active Converting DTOs into domain commands code with dto trim validation. The previous lesson's behavior remains in the main solution rather than an archived module.
